name: todays-goals
description: Morning ritual skill that combines an external briefing with structured goal-setting. Runs in four phases — calendar, email, deliverables, then open drill-down.
instructions:
Run a structured session in four phases. Each phase surfaces candidates; the user confirms each one before it enters the drill-down. Write results to ~/.notes-context/daily-goals.md.

## Phase 1 — Calendar

Use the Google Calendar MCP tool to list today's events. Authenticate if prompted. If the tool is unavailable or returns no events, skip this phase silently. If there are events, summarise them and ask which need prep work as a goal. For each confirmed event, run the drill-down and write to daily-goals.md.

## Phase 2 — Email

Read ~/.notes-context/daily-briefing/emails.md. If absent or empty, skip silently. For each email, apply judgment: always surface emails from Stakeholders (any name/address in ~/.notes-context/projects/*/stakeholders.md); surface others only if action-implied (reply, decide, send). Present surfaced emails one at a time; if user confirms, run drill-down and write to daily-goals.md.

## Phase 3 — Deliverables

Check ~/.notes-context/projects/*/roadmap.md. If no roadmap exists, skip silently. If one project has a roadmap, use it. If multiple projects have roadmaps, ask which project to focus on. Surface Now-horizon non-blocked Deliverables (blocked items carry blocked: in an HTML comment). For each confirmed Deliverable, run drill-down and write ## Deliverable name + - [ ] subtasks to daily-goals.md. roadmap.md is never modified by this skill.

## Phase 4 — Open drill-down

Ask: "Anything else you want to achieve today?"

For each goal stated, ask at least one follow-up question:
- "What does done look like for that?"
- "Any blockers or dependencies?"
- "Is there a specific first step?"

Use answers to form - [ ] subtasks. Keep subtasks action-oriented and completable within the day.

## Writing to daily-goals.md

Write results to ~/.notes-context/daily-goals.md. Format:

## Goal name
- [ ] Subtask one
- [ ] Subtask two

If the file already contains goals from an earlier session today, append new goals rather than overwriting. Do not duplicate any ## heading already present in the file.

## Notes

- Do not modify user-planner.md — that is the Retrospective Skill's concern.
- Do not read or reference previous days' notes — focus is on today only.
- daily-briefing/calendar.md and daily-briefing/emails.md are read-only inputs.
